steps.title: Identify the function output
steps.content: The problem says $s(t)$ gives the approximate mass, in grams, that remains after $t$ years. So $s(t)$ is remaining mass, not mass lost.

steps.title: Interpret the input and output
steps.content: In $s(62)=36$, the input $62$ means $62$ years after decay begins, and the output $36$ means $36$ grams remain. Therefore, the statement must say that about $36$ grams remain after $62$ years. The answer is A.
